-- 6 of 28 --

HOUSEKEEPING
• Going til about 5PM ET (both days)
• One-hour lunch break at 12:30PM, with a
morning and afternoon 10-minute bio break
each day
• Recordings & PDFs on Monday afternoon
• Extra Bonus: 3-hour open Question & Answer
Session (Monday - 5-8PM ET)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 28 --

WORKSHOP AGENDA
OVERVIEW
DAY ONE:
• 	Core Copy Principles
• 	Problem Buckets
• 	Problems vs. Pain Points
• 	3 Elements of Persuasion
• 	Benefit Types
• 	Avoiding Faux Benefits
• 	Value of Contrast
• 	Research & Decisions
• 	The “Offer Solution”
• 	Prospect Risks
• 	Buying Belief
• 	Hooks & Themes
• 	Lead Emotions
• 	Argument & Proof
DAY TWO:
• 	Word Selection
• 	Sentence Structure
• 	Writing Voice
• 	The Power Of Three’s
• 	Copy Flow
• 	Prospect Disarming
• 	Language Patterns
• 	Figures Of Speech
• 	Rhetorical Devices
• 	Framing
• 	Loaded Language
• 	Dirty Details
• 	Use of Dialogue
• 	Writing!
AND MUCH MORE!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 28 --

The Better The Offer…
The Weaker The Copy Can Be!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 28 --

FOUNDATIONAL
PRINCIPLES
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 28 --

People 	don’t 	buy for the
product, they buy for the
result, the change!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 28 --

They buy 	solutions 	to
problems 	they want resolved…
to 	feel 	how they want to feel!
People buy based on emotion… and they buy emotions.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 28 --

“Buying Emotions”
• Home Security System = Feel safe
• Botox = Feel more attractive
• Rolex = Feel more successful
• Teeth Whitening = Feel more confident
• Productivity System = Feel less overwhelmed
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 28 --

We 	don’t 	create the desire
to solve a problem,
we channel existing desire
to create demand for our offer.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 14 of 28 --

2 BUCKETS OF 	PROBLEMS
UNRESOLVED
DISTRESS
UNFULFILLED
NEED OR
DESIRE
PROBLEM = PAIN!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 15 of 28 --

The problem is 	NOT 	the
same as the pain…
The problem is the cause of the pain.
The pain is what the prospect
wants solved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 16 of 28 --

The pain comes from how
the prospect feels about
the problem.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 17 of 28 --

STRATEGIC AIM OF OUR COPY:
Show the prospect we have a
different 	and 	superior 	way to
solve their problem.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 18 of 28 --

STRATEGIC AIM OF OUR COPY:
Show how solving the problem
takes the prospect's pain away
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 19 of 28 --

CORE OF PERSUASION
ACCORDING TO ARISTOTLE
• Pathos: Emotional appeal
• Ethos: Character, reputation, identity
• Logos: Logical, rational reasoning
Good copy leverages the prospect’s core emotion… prods buying emotions…
appeals to their desired identity… and gives them the logical buying support.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 20 of 28 --

UNDERSTANDING BENEFITS
• 	Functional Benefit: The typical superficial benefit (A
mechanical pencil. The soft, spongy grip is a feature. The
functional benefit is that it’s easier to hold.)
• 	Dimensionalized Benefit: What the functional benefit actually
looks like in the real life of a prospect. Paint the picture. “You can
write for hours everyday with no pain in your hand or wrist.”
• 	Emotional Benefit: The emotional impact of the
dimensionalized benefit. How does the dimensionalized benefit
make them feel? “Feel great knowing that you can get up
everyday and write to your heart’s content without ever having
to worry about being in pain again.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 21 of 28 --

Dimensionalized &
Emotional Benefits Are How
You 	Create Mental Pictures
In Your Prospect’s Mind
You’re the director of the movie in the mind of the prospect.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 22 of 28 --

LEVERAGE 	MULTIPLE SENSES 	IN
YOUR BENEFIT DESCRIPTIONS
SIGHT 	SOUND 	TOUCH 	SMELL	TASTE
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 23 of 28 --

BEWARE 	“FAUX BENEFITS”
• A statement which, on the surface, feels like a
benefit... but, it's at least one step removed from
what the prospect wants.
• “Flush fat from your intestinal tract.”
• “Your arteries and blood vessels will instantly
relax.”
• “Make your ads more attractive.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 24 of 28 --

TURNING “FAUX BENEFITS”
INTO TO REAL BENEFITS
• So you can…
• So you will…
• So you…
• Which means, you…
• …and…
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 25 of 28 --

UNDERSTANDING CONTRAST
• The state of being strikingly different from
something else.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 26 of 28 --

UNDERSTANDING CONTRAST
• Every solution is made more valuable in the
context of the problem it solves.
• The more severe the problem is perceived to be,
the more valuable the solution is perceived to be.
• “Never again will you feel humiliated when you take
your shirt off at the beach. Instead, you’ll notice
woman looking… and men admiring… as you
confidently strut around with a six-pack.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 27 of 28 --

TERMS & DEFINITIONS
• Hook/Theme
• Campaign Lead
• Offer
• Campaign Argument
• Unique Selling Proposition
• Unique Mechanism
• Catalyst
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 28 of 28 --
